description: Analyse term structure — spot, forward, par yields, swap curve, traditional theories (expectations, liquidity, segmented, preferred habitat), yield curve factor models, key rate duration

spot_forward_relationships.spot_rate: Yield on zero-coupon bond maturing at time t
spot_forward_relationships.forward_rate: Implied future spot rate from no-arbitrage
spot_forward_relationships.ytm_relationship: YTM is complex average of spots over coupon dates

yield_curve_movement.forward_curve: Realised future rates differ from forwards if expectations or premia change
yield_curve_movement.rolling_yield: Total return = coupon + roll-down + price change

active_management.riding_yield_curve: Buy long, hold as it rolls down

swap_rate_curve.definition: Fixed rate on par swap; benchmark vs treasuries
swap_rate_curve.why_used: More liquid in many markets; reflects bank credit

traditional_theories.expectations: Forward rates equal expected future spots; no premium
traditional_theories.liquidity_preference: Forwards = expected spot + positive liquidity premium increasing with maturity
traditional_theories.segmented_markets: Yields determined within maturity segments by supply/demand
traditional_theories.preferred_habitat: Investors leave preferred maturity only for sufficient premium

key_rate_duration.definition: Sensitivity to a single key rate, holding others constant
key_rate_duration.use_for_non_parallel_shift: Decompose curve risk by maturity bucket

errors.message: analysis_type must be one of the supported types

anti_patterns.rule: Do not interpret forward rates as best forecasts of future spots
anti_patterns.why: Under liquidity preference theory forwards include a term premium; treating forwards as unbiased forecasts mis-prices expectations of policy and growth

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use effective duration alone for non-parallel shifts
anti_patterns.why: Effective duration assumes parallel shift; real curve moves include slope and curvature changes — key rate duration is required to capture them

anti_patterns.rule: Do not rely on a single yield curve theory
anti_patterns.why: Empirical evidence supports a blend of expectations, liquidity, and preferred habitat; rigid adherence to one theory mis-prices the curve
